grants.target_population: Researchers and institutions conducting health-related research
grants.description: The NIH R01 grant supports health-related research and development based on the mission of the NIH. It is intended for projects that are of high scientific caliber.
grants.notes: Requires detailed project proposal and budget justification.

grants.target_population: Communities seeking to integrate arts into community revitalization
grants.description: Our Town grants support projects that integrate arts, culture, and design into local efforts to strengthen communities.
grants.notes: Partnerships between arts organizations and local government are required.

grants.target_population: Schools and organizations aiming to improve access to local foods
grants.description: The Farm to School Grant Program assists in implementing farm to school programs that improve access to local foods in schools.
grants.notes: Matching funds are required.

grants.target_population: Educators and organizations focused on environmental education
grants.description: The EPA's Environmental Education Grants program supports projects that enhance the public's awareness, knowledge, and skills to make informed decisions that affect environmental quality.
grants.notes: Projects must address EPA's educational priorities.

grants.target_population: Low-income households needing energy efficiency improvements
grants.description: The Weatherization Assistance Program provides funding to states to improve the energy efficiency of low-income homes, reducing energy costs for families in need.
grants.notes: Funds are distributed to states, which then allocate to local agencies.

grants.opportunity_title: Rural Health Care Services Outreach Program
grants.target_population: Rural communities needing improved healthcare access
grants.description: This program supports projects that demonstrate innovative models to improve healthcare services in rural communities.
grants.notes: Projects must be community-based and involve a consortium of local partners.

grants.provider: U.S. Department of Housing and Urban Development (HUD)
grants.target_population: Low and moderate-income communities
grants.description: The CDBG program provides annual grants to states, cities, and counties to develop viable urban communities by providing decent housing and a suitable living environment, and by expanding economic opportunities.
grants.notes: Applicants must ensure compliance with national objectives and citizen participation requirements.

grants.opportunity_title: Homeless Housing, Assistance and Prevention Program
grants.provider: California Business, Consumer Services and Housing Agency
grants.target_population: Homeless individuals and families in California
grants.description: This program provides funds to support regional coordination and expand or develop local capacity to address immediate homelessness challenges.
grants.notes: Requires a local plan to address homelessness and a commitment to regional collaboration.

grants.target_population: Communities in need of criminal justice and public safety improvements
grants.description: The program provides funding to support projects that enhance the criminal justice system, improve public safety, and reduce crime in Texas communities.
grants.notes: Applicants must demonstrate a clear need and provide measurable outcomes.

grants.target_population: Arts organizations and cultural programs in Florida
grants.description: This grant supports the general program activities of arts and cultural organizations, enhancing access to cultural experiences across Florida.
grants.notes: Applicants must demonstrate a history of cultural programming and community engagement.

grants.target_population: Communities seeking to improve transportation infrastructure
grants.description: TAP provides funding for community-based projects that expand travel choices and enhance transportation infrastructure, including pedestrian and bicycle facilities.
grants.notes: Projects must align with state transportation goals and demonstrate community support.

grants.provider: Georgia Emergency Management and Homeland Security Agency
grants.target_population: Communities requiring enhanced emergency management capabilities
grants.description: EMPG provides funding to assist local and state governments in preparing for all hazards through planning, training, and exercises.
grants.notes: Requires a 50% match from non-federal sources and adherence to FEMA guidelines.

grants.target_population: Populations in need of health interventions globally
grants.description: This program supports innovative solutions to improve health outcomes in low-income countries. Funding focuses on disease prevention and health system strengthening.
grants.notes: Proposals must align with the foundation's strategic priorities.

grants.target_population: Communities seeking to integrate arts into community revitalization
grants.description: This grant program supports projects that integrate arts and culture into community development efforts. It aims to enhance the quality of life and economic vitality of communities.
grants.notes: Projects must demonstrate community engagement and sustainability.

grants.target_population: Organizations with transformative solutions to critical social challenges
grants.description: 100&Change is a competition for a $100 million grant to fund a single proposal that promises real and measurable progress in solving a critical problem of our time.
grants.notes: Open to organizations worldwide; proposals must be impactful and feasible.

grants.target_population: Youth involved in the juvenile justice system
grants.description: This program supports efforts to reform juvenile justice systems to improve outcomes for youth. It focuses on reducing incarceration and promoting community-based alternatives.
grants.notes: Preference given to initiatives that demonstrate measurable outcomes.

grants.target_population: Underserved communities facing health disparities
grants.description: This program funds initiatives that address health disparities and promote health equity in underserved communities. It supports innovative approaches to improving community health.
grants.notes: Projects must align with the foundation's vision for a Culture of Health.

grants.target_population: Children and families in need of educational support
grants.description: This program supports initiatives that improve educational outcomes for children, particularly in early childhood. It aims to ensure equitable access to quality education.
grants.notes: Focus on projects that demonstrate community involvement and sustainability.

grants.target_population: Nonprofits leveraging technology for social impact
grants.description: The Google.org Impact Challenge supports nonprofits using technology to tackle societal challenges. Grants are awarded to innovative projects that have the potential to create significant community impact.
grants.notes: Projects should demonstrate a clear plan for execution and scalability.

grants.target_population: Organizations using AI to address societal challenges
grants.description: Microsoft's AI for Good program offers grants to organizations using artificial intelligence to solve pressing social issues. The program provides funding and resources to help scale AI-driven solutions.
grants.notes: Applicants must demonstrate the ethical use of AI in their projects.

grants.target_population: Communities seeking economic revitalization
grants.description: The AdvancingCities Challenge by JPMorgan Chase supports initiatives aimed at driving inclusive growth and economic opportunity in cities. The program funds innovative solutions that address workforce development and economic revitalization.
grants.notes: Preference given to projects with collaborative partnerships.

grants.target_population: Local nonprofits focusing on community development
grants.description: The Neighborhood Builders Program provides leadership development and flexible funding to nonprofits working to address economic mobility and community development in their local areas.
grants.notes: Includes leadership training for executive directors and emerging leaders.

grants.target_population: Nonprofits serving communities in the Minneapolis-St. Paul area
grants.description: The Target Foundation Hometown Grant supports local nonprofits in the Minneapolis-St. Paul area, focusing on arts, culture, and social services. The program aims to enhance community vitality and well-being.
grants.notes: Priority given to organizations with a strong community presence.

grants.target_population: Local communities in need of hunger relief and community support
grants.description: The Walmart Foundation Community Grant Program provides funding to local organizations working to address hunger relief, community engagement, and other critical needs in their communities.
grants.notes: Applications are accepted on a rolling basis throughout the year.

grants.target_population: Populations experiencing health disparities
grants.description: This grant supports research aimed at understanding and addressing health disparities among different populations in the United States.
grants.notes: Applicants must demonstrate expertise in health disparities research.

grants.opportunity_title: Robert Wood Johnson Foundation Culture of Health Prize
grants.target_population: Communities working towards health equity
grants.description: The Culture of Health Prize honors communities that are working to build a culture of health for all residents.
grants.notes: Communities must demonstrate measurable progress in health equity.

grants.opportunity_title: California Mental Health Services Act (MHSA) Grants
grants.provider: California Department of Health Care Services
grants.target_population: California residents with mental health needs
grants.description: MHSA provides funding for mental health services and programs in California, focusing on prevention and early intervention.
grants.notes: Programs must align with state mental health priorities.

grants.target_population: Underserved communities in the U.S.
grants.description: This program supports efforts to improve the health of low-income, underserved communities by addressing social determinants of health.
grants.notes: Proposals must demonstrate innovative approaches to health improvement.

grants.opportunity_title: Texas Homeless Housing and Services Program (HHSP)
grants.provider: Texas Department of Housing and Community Affairs
grants.target_population: Homeless individuals and families in Texas
grants.description: HHSP provides funding to support services and housing solutions for homeless individuals and families in Texas.
grants.notes: Applicants must demonstrate capacity to manage housing programs.

grants.target_population: Children in Florida needing improved healthcare access
grants.description: This program provides grants to improve healthcare access for children in Florida, focusing on underserved areas.
grants.notes: Projects must align with state health priorities for children.

grants.target_population: Artists and arts organizations across the United States
grants.description: The Art Works grant program supports projects that celebrate creativity and cultural heritage, and that engage the public with diverse art forms. Funding is available for a wide range of artistic disciplines.
grants.notes: Matching funds are required.

grants.opportunity_title: Kresge Foundation Arts and Culture Program
grants.target_population: Communities seeking to integrate arts and culture into community revitalization
grants.description: The Kresge Foundation's Arts and Culture Program supports efforts that integrate arts and culture into community development and revitalization efforts, with a focus on creative placemaking.
grants.notes: Proposals are accepted on a rolling basis.

grants.target_population: Florida-based arts organizations and cultural programs
grants.description: This grant provides funding to Florida arts and cultural organizations to support general program operations, including arts education and cultural programming.

grants.target_population: Texas communities and underserved populations
grants.description: The Arts Respond Project grant provides funding for arts-based projects that address community needs and engage underserved populations in Texas.
grants.notes: Projects must address one of the priority areas outlined by the commission.

grants.target_population: Chicago-based arts organizations and initiatives
grants.description: The MacArthur Foundation supports innovative arts and culture organizations in the Chicago area, focusing on projects that promote cultural diversity and artistic innovation.
grants.notes: Focus on organizations with a strong track record of impact in the community.

grants.target_population: Seattle-based arts organizations and community groups
grants.description: The Civic Partners Grant supports Seattle-based arts organizations and community groups that contribute to the city's cultural vitality and community building through arts and culture.
grants.notes: Organizations must demonstrate a commitment to community engagement.

grants.target_population: Small businesses engaged in scientific research and development
grants.description: The NSF SBIR program provides funding to small businesses to support research and development of innovative technologies that have the potential for commercialization.
grants.notes: Phase I and Phase II funding available; requires commercialization plan.

grants.target_population: Innovators working on global health and development challenges
grants.description: This program funds innovative ideas to solve pressing global health and development problems. It encourages bold and unconventional approaches.
grants.notes: Initial grants are seed funding; follow-on funding available for successful projects.

grants.target_population: Organizations developing energy innovations in California
grants.description: EPIC funds research, development, and demonstration projects that advance clean energy technologies and support California's energy policy goals.
grants.notes: Projects must align with California's energy policy goals; matching funds may be required.

grants.target_population: Technology companies and research institutions in Texas
grants.description: The Texas Emerging Technology Fund provides funding to support the development and commercialization of new technologies in Texas.
grants.notes: Focus on projects with high potential for commercialization and job creation.

grants.target_population: Innovators developing new tools and approaches in media and journalism
grants.description: The Prototype Fund supports early-stage media and information projects that explore new ways to inform and engage communities.
grants.notes: Focus on rapid prototyping and testing of new ideas in media and journalism.

grants.target_population: Educators and community organizations
grants.description: The EPA's Environmental Education Grants Program supports projects that enhance the public's awareness, knowledge, and skills to make informed decisions that affect environmental quality.
grants.notes: Projects must include an educational component and demonstrate measurable results.

grants.target_population: Communities in California affected by climate change
grants.description: This program funds projects that reduce greenhouse gas emissions and provide economic, environmental, and public health benefits to disadvantaged communities in California.
grants.notes: Projects must align with California's climate goals and demonstrate community benefits.

grants.target_population: Florida communities and ecosystems
grants.description: Florida Forever is the state's premier conservation and recreation lands acquisition program, focusing on protecting natural resources and wildlife habitats.
grants.notes: Applicants must demonstrate the ecological significance of the land to be conserved.

grants.target_population: Organizations working on biodiversity conservation
grants.description: The MacArthur Foundation supports efforts to conserve biodiversity and promote sustainable development in critical regions around the world.
grants.notes: Focus on innovative solutions and partnerships in biodiversity hotspots.

grants.target_population: Ohio educators and community organizations
grants.description: This fund supports projects that increase awareness and understanding of environmental issues and encourage the adoption of sustainable practices in Ohio.
grants.notes: Applicants must demonstrate measurable environmental outcomes.

grants.target_population: Communities and organizations focused on sustainability
grants.description: The Walmart Foundation provides funding to support initiatives that promote environmental sustainability, including waste reduction and sustainable agriculture.
grants.notes: Preference given to projects with scalable and replicable solutions.

grants.target_population: Students and educators worldwide
grants.description: The Global Learning Initiative supports innovative education projects that aim to improve learning outcomes for students globally. The program funds scalable solutions that can be adapted to different educational contexts.
grants.notes: Projects must demonstrate scalability and potential for global impact.

grants.target_population: Florida high school graduates
grants.description: The Florida Bright Futures Scholarship Program provides scholarships based on high school academic achievement. It is designed to help students attend postsecondary institutions in Florida.
grants.notes: Students must maintain a certain GPA to retain the scholarship.

grants.target_population: Students and educators in arts programs
grants.description: The Arts Education Grant supports projects that help children and youth acquire knowledge and understanding of the arts. The program funds projects that integrate arts education into the school curriculum.
grants.notes: Matching funds are required for this grant.

grants.target_population: Students in high-need communities
grants.description: This program provides funding for academic enrichment opportunities during non-school hours for children, particularly students who attend high-poverty and low-performing schools.
grants.notes: Programs must operate during non-school hours or periods when school is not in session.

grants.target_population: Youth aged 6-24
grants.description: YouthSpark aims to empower young people by providing access to computer science education and digital skills training. The program supports initiatives that help youth succeed in a technology-driven world.
grants.notes: Focus on underserved communities and promoting diversity in tech.

grants.target_population: Researchers and educators in Ohio
grants.description: The Ohio Education Research Grant supports research projects that aim to improve educational outcomes in the state. The program encourages innovative approaches to educational challenges.
grants.notes: Research findings must be shared with the Ohio Department of Education.

grants.target_population: Rural communities in Texas
grants.description: This program provides financial assistance to rural communities in Texas to improve public infrastructure and encourage economic development.
grants.notes: Projects must create or retain permanent jobs.

grants.opportunity_title: Florida Small Cities Community Development Block Grant
grants.target_population: Small cities and rural areas in Florida
grants.description: This program provides funding to small cities and rural areas in Florida for community development projects that improve housing, public facilities, and economic opportunities.
grants.notes: Projects must benefit low- and moderate-income persons.

grants.target_population: Communities needing systemic change solutions
grants.description: 100&Change is a competition for a $100 million grant to fund a single proposal that promises real and measurable progress in solving a critical problem of our time.
grants.notes: Highly competitive; requires a detailed proposal and plan for systemic change.
